title: Dozens of Robotaxi Riders Are Falling Asleep, Sparking Frantic Calls For Emergency Services - Slashdot
excerpt: "If tired or wasted passengers fall asleep in a traditional taxi or rideshare, the driver can shout or shake them awake," reports Bloomberg. "Not so in a robotaxi..." Ditto Kasendar remembers soft music drifting from the robotaxi's speakers as he rode home late at night from a friend's birthday pa...
content_html:
"If tired or wasted passengers fall asleep in a traditional taxi or rideshare, the driver can shout or shake them awake," reports Bloomberg. "Not so in a robotaxi..." Ditto Kasendar remembers soft music drifting from the robotaxi's speakers as he rode home late at night from a friend's birthday party in 2025. The next moment, Los Angeles firefighters were opening the door and asking if he was OK. His six-minute trip had ended nearly an hour before. A remote Waymo assistant, dialling in through the car's speakers, repeatedly tried to rouse him and finally called 911 when he wouldn't stir...

Kasendar's robocab nap was, unfortunately, not an isolated incident. As companies like Alphabet and Tesla bring self-driving taxis to more cities, the messier aspects of serving unpredictable humans are becoming harder to ignore. Passengers are falling asleep, spilling drinks, dropping food, vomiting, experiencing medical emergencies and, in at least two instances, giving birth in the cars. They stumble out of the vehicles and forget to close the doors, forcing the operators to pay nearby gig workers to do it.

These seemingly minor nuisances are becoming a drain on municipal resources and complicating the roll-out of robotaxi service. So many robotaxi customers have nodded off in the midst of a ride that Austin police and firefighters even have a name for the incidents: "sleepers". The Texas capital recorded 99 such calls in Waymo's first nine months of service there, said Roger Patterson, a commander with Austin-Travis County Emergency Medical Services... Remote assistants monitoring the cars try talking through the speakers and checking on passengers with interior cameras. But if they get no response, company protocols often require them to call 911. And first responders have to assume the worst. Austin dispatchers treat an incident as a potential heart attack if the remote assistant can't tell whether the passenger is breathing, Patterson said. In the end, only about 3% of such calls require transporting the passenger to a hospital, he said. But the incidents tie up personnel who might be needed elsewhere.

So many robotaxi customers have nodded off in the midst of a ride that Austin police and firefighters even have a name for the incidents: "sleepers". And first responders have to assume the worst. Austin dispatchers treat an incident as a potential heart attack if the remote assistant can't tell whether the passenger is breathing, Patterson said. In the end, only about 3% of such calls require transporting the passenger to a hospital, he said. But the incidents tie up personnel who might be needed elsewhere.

"If tired or wasted passengers fall asleep in a traditional taxi or rideshare, the driver can shout or shake them awake," reports Bloomberg. "Not so in a robotaxi..." Ditto Kasendar remembers soft music drifting from the robotaxi's speakers as he rode home late at night from a friend's birthday party in 2025. The next moment, Los Angeles firefighters were opening the door and asking if he was OK. His six-minute trip had ended nearly an hour before. A remote Waymo assistant, dialling in through the car's speakers, repeatedly tried to rouse him and finally called 911 when he wouldn't stir...

Kasendar's robocab nap was, unfortunately, not an isolated incident. As companies like Alphabet and Tesla bring self-driving taxis to more cities, the messier aspects of serving unpredictable humans are becoming harder to ignore. Passengers are falling asleep, spilling drinks, dropping food, vomiting, experiencing medical emergencies and, in at least two instances, giving birth in the cars. These seemingly minor nuisances are becoming a drain on municipal resources and complicating the roll-out of robotaxi service. And first responders have to assume the worst. Austin dispatchers treat an incident as a potential heart attack if the remote assistant can't tell whether the passenger is breathing, Patterson said. sentences.en: "If tired or wasted passengers fall asleep in a traditional taxi or rideshare, the driver can shout or shake them awake," reports Bloomberg.
sentences.zh: 彭博社报道称：“如果疲惫或疲惫的乘客在传统出租车或拼车中睡着了，司机可以大声喊叫或摇醒他们。”

sentences.en: "Not so in a robotaxi..."
sentences.zh: “在机器人出租车里却不是这样……”

sentences.en: Ditto Kasendar remembers soft music drifting from the robotaxi's speakers as he rode home late at night from a friend's birthday party in 2025.
sentences.zh: Ditto Kasendar 记得 2025 年，当他参加完朋友的生日聚会后，深夜骑车回家时，机器人出租车的扬声器里飘出轻柔的音乐。

sentences.en: The next moment, Los Angeles firefighters were opening the door and asking if he was OK.
sentences.zh: 下一刻，洛杉矶消防队员打开门询问他是否还好。

sentences.en: His six-minute trip had ended nearly an hour before.
sentences.zh: 他六分钟的旅程在将近一个小时前就结束了。

sentences.en: A remote Waymo assistant, dialling in through the car's speakers, repeatedly tried to rouse him and finally called 911 when he wouldn't stir...
sentences.zh: 一名远程 Waymo 助理通过汽车扬声器拨通电话，反复试图叫醒他，最后在他不动的情况下拨打了 911……

sentences.en: Kasendar's robocab nap was, unfortunately, not an isolated incident.
sentences.zh: 不幸的是，卡森达尔的机器人出租车小睡并不是一个孤立的事件。

sentences.en: As companies like Alphabet and Tesla bring self-driving taxis to more cities, the messier aspects of serving unpredictable humans are becoming harder to ignore.
sentences.zh: 随着 Alphabet 和特斯拉等公司将自动驾驶出租车带到更多城市，为不可预测的人类提供服务的混乱方面正变得越来越难以忽视。

sentences.en: Passengers are falling asleep, spilling drinks, dropping food, vomiting, experiencing medical emergencies and, in at least two instances, giving birth in the cars.
sentences.zh: 乘客们会睡着、洒出饮料、掉落食物、呕吐、经历医疗紧急情况，并且至少有两次在车内分娩。

sentences.en: They stumble out of the vehicles and forget to close the doors, forcing the operators to pay nearby gig workers to do it.
sentences.zh: 他们跌跌撞撞地下了车，忘记关门，迫使运营商付钱给附近的零工来做这件事。

sentences.en: These seemingly minor nuisances are becoming a drain on municipal resources and complicating the roll-out of robotaxi service.
sentences.zh: 这些看似微不足道的问题正在消耗市政资源，并使机器人出租车服务的推出变得更加复杂。

sentences.en: So many robotaxi customers have nodded off in the midst of a ride that Austin police and firefighters even have a name for the incidents: "sleepers".
sentences.zh: 如此多的机器人出租车乘客在乘车过程中打瞌睡，奥斯汀警方和消防队员甚至为这些事件起了一个名字：“沉睡者”。

sentences.en: The Texas capital recorded 99 such calls in Waymo's first nine months of service there, said Roger Patterson, a commander with Austin-Travis County Emergency Medical Services...
sentences.zh: 奥斯汀-特拉维斯县紧急医疗服务中心指挥官罗杰·帕特森 (Roger Patterson) 表示，在 Waymo 服务的前 9 个月里，得克萨斯州首府就记录了 99 个此类电话。

sentences.en: Remote assistants monitoring the cars try talking through the speakers and checking on passengers with interior cameras.
sentences.zh: 监控汽车的远程助手尝试通过扬声器进行交谈，并使用车内摄像头检查乘客的情况。

sentences.en: But if they get no response, company protocols often require them to call 911.
sentences.zh: 但如果他们没有得到回应，公司规定通常要求他们拨打 911。

sentences.en: And first responders have to assume the worst.
sentences.zh: 急救人员必须做最坏的打算。

sentences.en: Austin dispatchers treat an incident as a potential heart attack if the remote assistant can't tell whether the passenger is breathing, Patterson said.
sentences.zh: 帕特森说，如果远程助理无法判断乘客是否有呼吸，奥斯汀调度员会将事件视为潜在的心脏病发作。

sentences.en: In the end, only about 3% of such calls require transporting the passenger to a hospital, he said.
sentences.zh: 他说，最终，只有大约 3% 的此类电话需要将乘客送往医院。

sentences.en: But the incidents tie up personnel who might be needed elsewhere.
sentences.zh: 但这些事件束缚了其他地方可能需要的人员。

They stumble out of the vehicles and forget to close the doors, forcing the operators to pay nearby gig workers to do it.

sentences.zh: 他们跌跌撞撞地下了车，忘记关门，迫使运营商付钱给附近的零工来做这件事。

And first responders have to assume the worst. Austin dispatchers treat an incident as a potential heart attack if the remote assistant can't tell whether the passenger is breathing, Patterson said. In the end, only about 3% of such calls require transporting the passenger to a hospital, he said. But the incidents tie up personnel who might be needed elsewhere.

sentences.zh: 如此多的机器人出租车乘客在乘车过程中打瞌睡，奥斯汀警方和消防队员甚至为这些事件起了一个名字：“沉睡者”。

sentences.zh: 奥斯汀-特拉维斯县紧急医疗服务中心指挥官罗杰·帕特森 (Roger Patterson) 表示，在 Waymo 服务的前 9 个月里，得克萨斯州首府就记录了 99 个此类电话。

sentences.zh: 监控汽车的远程助手尝试通过扬声器进行交谈，并使用车内摄像头检查乘客的情况。

sentences.en: But if they get no response, company protocols often require them to call 911.
sentences.zh: 但如果他们没有得到回应，公司规定通常要求他们拨打 911。

sentences.en: And first responders have to assume the worst.
sentences.zh: 急救人员必须做最坏的打算。

sentences.zh: 帕特森说，如果远程助理无法判断乘客是否有呼吸，奥斯汀调度员会将事件视为潜在的心脏病发作。

sentences.zh: 他说，最终，只有大约 3% 的此类电话需要将乘客送往医院。

sentences.en: But the incidents tie up personnel who might be needed elsewhere.
sentences.zh: 但这些事件束缚了其他地方可能需要的人员。

content_html_zh: 彭博社报道称：“如果疲惫或疲惫的乘客在传统出租车或拼车中睡着了，司机可以大声喊叫或摇醒他们。” “在机器人出租车里却不是这样……” Ditto Kasendar 记得 2025 年，当他参加完朋友的生日聚会后，深夜骑车回家时，机器人出租车的扬声器里飘出轻柔的音乐。 下一刻，洛杉矶消防队员打开门询问他是否还好。 他六分钟的旅程在将近一个小时前就结束了。 一名远程 Waymo 助理通过汽车扬声器拨通电话，反复试图叫醒他，最后在他不动的情况下拨打了 911…… 不幸的是，卡森达尔的机器人出租车小睡并不是一个孤立的事件。 随着 Alphabet 和特斯拉等公司将自动驾驶出租车带到更多城市，为不可预测的人类提供服务的混乱方面正变得越来越难以忽视。 乘客们会睡着、洒出饮料、掉落食物、呕吐、经历医疗紧急情况，并且至少有两次在车内分娩。 他们跌跌撞撞地下了车，忘记关门，迫使运营商付钱给附近的零工来做这件事。 这些看似微不足道的问题正在消耗市政资源，并使机器人出租车服务的推出变得更加复杂。 如此多的机器人出租车乘客在乘车过程中打瞌睡，奥斯汀警方和消防队员甚至为这些事件起了一个名字：“沉睡者”。 奥斯汀-特拉维斯县紧急医疗服务中心指挥官罗杰·帕特森 (Roger Patterson) 表示，在 Waymo 服务的前 9 个月里，得克萨斯州首府就记录了 99 个此类电话。 监控汽车的远程助手尝试通过扬声器进行交谈，并使用车内摄像头检查乘客的情况。 但如果他们没有得到回应，公司规定通常要求他们拨打 911。 急救人员必须做最坏的打算。 帕特森说，如果远程助理无法判断乘客是否有呼吸，奥斯汀调度员会将事件视为潜在的心脏病发作。 他说，最终，只有大约 3% 的此类电话需要将乘客送往医院。 但这些事件束缚了其他地方可能需要的人员。
